title: Field-Level Validation Metadata for All Core Entities
description: The system shall maintain field-level validation metadata for all core Group Benefits data entities: Employer/Group Profile, Employee, Dependent, Plan, Enrollment, Life Event, Carrier, Payroll, and EDI 834 transaction.

architectureDecision.principle: Use configurable field metadata rather than hardcoded validation logic
architectureDecision.aiRequirementsStory: AI ingests Benefits Guide → AI proposes field validation rule → Human approves → Rule registered in validation engine → Application enforces rule → AI traces rule back to source document.

title: Required, Optional, and Conditional Field Classification
description: The system shall classify each field as Required, Optional, or Conditional. Conditional fields specify the condition that makes them required.

classifications.REQUIRED: Field must be present and non-empty
classifications.OPTIONAL: Field may be absent or empty
classifications.CONDITIONAL: Required when a specified condition is met (e.g., Termination Date required when Employment Status = TERMINATED)

title: Data Type, Format, Length, and Allowable Value Validation
description: The system shall validate data type (string, integer, decimal, boolean, date, enum), format (regex pattern where applicable), field length (min/max characters), and allowable values (enum sets).

validationCategories.example: State must be valid US state/territory

title: Numeric Minimum and Maximum Range Validation
description: The system shall validate numeric minimum and maximum ranges where applicable. Employee Count must be greater than 0. Deduction amount must be ≥ 0. Work hours per week must be 0–168.

title: Date Format and Relationship Validation
description: The system shall validate date field formats and date relationships. Cross-date rules: Termination Date ≥ Effective Date; Hire Date ≤ current date; Coverage Effective Date within configured eligibility rules.

crossDateRules.rule: Enrollment Effective Date per eligibility rules

title: Configurable Regex Validation for Structured Fields
description: The system shall support configurable regular-expression validation for structured fields such as EIN, SSN, ZIP Code, email address, and phone number. Patterns shall be stored in field metadata and not hardcoded.

title: Conditional Validation Based on Other Field Values
description: The system shall support conditional validation rules that activate based on other field values. Examples: SSN required when employee is US-based; Termination Date required when Employment Status = TERMINATED; Life Event required when Enrollment Type = LIFE_EVENT.

conditionalExamples.then: Life Event Type and Event Date are required
DOB must satisfy configured dependent age eligibility rule — not hardcoded age 26

title: Cross-Field Validation Rules
description: The system shall support cross-field validation rules that compare values across multiple fields within the same entity. Cross-field validation occurs after individual field validation passes.

crossFieldRules.rule: Termination Date must be ≥ Effective Date
Termination Date must be ≥ Hire Date
Termination Date must be ≥ Effective Date
Enrollment Effective Date must comply with eligibility waiting period rule
Life Event enrollment date within configured window for event type
Payroll Employee ID must map to a valid Employee ID

title: Referential Integrity Validation for Key Identifiers
description: The system shall validate referential integrity for key identifiers: Employee ID must exist in the employee record store; Dependent ID must be associated with the claimed employee; Plan ID must exist in the configured plan catalog; Carrier ID must exist in the carrier configuration; Payroll Employee ID must map to a valid employee.

title: Separation of Field-Level and Business-Rule Validation
description: The system shall separate field-level validation (type, format, length, range, cross-field) from business-rule validation (eligibility criteria, enrollment windows, plan rules, carrier rules). Field validation executes first; business rules execute only on structurally valid data.
validationPipeline: 7. Business rule evaluation (eligibility, enrollment window, plan rules)

title: Standardized Validation Error Codes and Human-Readable Messages
description: The system shall return standardized validation error codes and human-readable messages for all validation failures. Each error shall include: field name, error code, severity (ERROR or WARNING), human-readable message, and the rejected value.

singleError.message: EIN must be in XX-XXXXXXX format.

title: Prevent Invalid Records from Persisting or Transmitting
description: The system shall prevent records that fail required field-level or business-rule validation from being persisted to the data store or transmitted to downstream systems (carrier, payroll, EDI). Validation errors shall block the operation and return errors to the caller.

title: Server-Side Validation for All API Submissions
description: The system shall perform server-side validation for all data submitted through APIs, independent of client-side validation. Client-side validation provides immediate UX feedback; server-side validation is authoritative.

validationTiming.purpose: Authoritative — always validates regardless of UI

title: Configurable Validation Rules by Employer, Plan, Carrier, and Product Type
description: The system shall support configurable validation rules per employer group, plan, carrier, and product type where applicable. Dependent SSN requirements vary by carrier. Premium contribution limits may vary by employer. Dependent age cutoff is configured, not hardcoded.
configurableRuleExamples: Dependent SSN required per carrier configuration — not universal
Dependent child max age rule from eligibility config — not hardcoded 26
Premium calculation formula per payroll frequency — not fixed divisor
Enrollment window duration per life event type — not universal 30 days

title: Audit Trail When Validation Rules Are Changed
description: The system shall maintain an audit trail when validation rules are added, modified, or deactivated. Audit record includes: rule ID, entity, field, prior configuration, new configuration, changed-by actor, and timestamp. Links to REQ-AUD-001.
